---
title: "Other"
---

## Adding A Scan Input Source

In a scan process, you can import files you physically scan into your Hyland Capture system.

To add an input source for a scan process:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  In the Input Source pane on the right side of the layout, click Add Source. The Choose Your Source page is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  Select a scanner in the Physical Sources section. This section shows all the physical scanning devices connected to your workstation.
5.  Click Next. The Edit Input Source page is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
6.  Enter a name for the scan input source in the Source Name field. This name is displayed so you can identify this input source. The name of the scanner is entered by default.
7.  To automatically delete any blank pages as they are scanned into the system, select Automatically delete blank pages.
8.  To create a separate document each time a blank page is scanned into the system, select Create separate documents on blank pages. The document is separated before each blank page. If blank pages are not set to be automatically deleted, each one becomes the first page of the subsequent document.
9.  To adjust the tolerance for markings on blank pages, change the noise tolerance level. This determines how much noise is permissible to still read the pages as blank. Noise may include markings on a physical page, poor captured image quality, or debris on the scanner. If a page's detected noise level is greater than the noise tolerance setting, the page is not read as blank.
To change the noise tolerance level, move the Noise tolerance for captured pages slider to Low, Medium (default setting), or High.

Note:
The Noise tolerance for captured pages slider can only be adjusted when the Automatically delete blank pages or Create separate documents on blank pages option is selected.

10.  Click Next. The settings dialog box of the scanner is displayed.  Note:
The scanner settings dialog box may be displayed behind the Finish Configuring Hardware Settings page. The scanner settings must be configured before this page is enabled.

11.  Configure the scanner's settings (for example, color mode, resolution, and orientation). See the scanner's documentation for more information on the available settings.  Tip:
If any of the scan input source's blank page detection options is selected, disable any of the scanner driver's blank page detection settings to avoid potential conflicts.

12.  Save the scanner settings and close the settings dialog box. The Finish Configuring Hardware Settings page is enabled.
13.  Click Next. The Success! page is displayed.
14.  Click Finish.

## Adding A Sweep Input Source

In a sweep process, you can import single-page image, multi-page TIFF, or PDF files from a local or network directory into your Hyland Capture system.

To add an input source for a sweep process:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  In the Input Source pane on the right side of the layout, click Add Source. The Choose Your Source page is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  Select Sweep in the Digital Sources section, and then click Next. The Edit Input Source page is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Enter a name for the sweep input source in the Source Name field. This name is displayed so you can identify this input source.
6.  Click Browse to locate and select the file path of your sweep directory.
7.  Select one of the following options in the File to Document Conversion section:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Create separate documents per file (Default) | Creates a separate document for each file that is swept into the system. |
| Combine all files into single document | Creates a single document from all files that are swept into the system. |

8.  To automatically delete any blank pages as they are swept into the system, select Automatically delete blank pages.
9.  To create a separate document each time a blank page is swept into the system, select Create separate documents on blank pages. The document is separated before each blank page. If blank pages are not set to be automatically deleted, each one becomes the first page of the subsequent document.  Note:
The Create separate documents on blank pages option can only be selected when the Combine all files into single document option is selected.

10.  To adjust the tolerance for markings on blank pages, change the noise tolerance level. This determines how much noise is permissible to still read the pages as blank. Noise may include markings on a physical page, poor captured image quality, or debris on the scanner. If a page's detected noise level is greater than the noise tolerance setting, the page is not read as blank.
To change the noise tolerance level, move the Noise tolerance for captured pages slider to Low, Medium (default setting), or High.

Note:
The Noise tolerance for captured pages slider can only be adjusted when the Automatically delete blank pages or Create separate documents on blank pages option is selected.

11.  To move the local copies of your source files to a subfolder once they are swept into the system and submitted for verification, select Move source files to a "Processed" subfolder.
12.  To delete the local copies of your source files once they are swept into the system and submitted for verification, select Delete local files once swept into the system.  Note:
The Delete local files once swept into the system option can only be selected when the Move source files to a "Processed" subfolder option is selected.

13.  Click Next. The Success! page is displayed.
14.  Click Finish.

## Adding An Input Source

You can add a new sweep or scan input source to use for the batch capture process. All configured input sources are available for selection in the Input Source pane and Input Source drop-down list.

You can add a sweep input source to import files you can already access from a directory into your Hyland Capture system. You can add a scan input source to import files you must physically scan into your Hyland Capture system.

See the following sections for details:

-
Adding a Sweep Input Source

-
Adding a Scan Input Source

## Capture Rest Api Overview

The Hyland Capture REST API (Capture REST API) allows you to automate and streamline various processes, such as exporting batches, documents, and metadata from the web application.

## Changing The Display Density

Change the display density of the Hyland Capture application to adjust the spacing between such elements as text, fields, and buttons.

To change the display density:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application).
2.  Select the user menu icon in the upper-right corner of the interface to expand the user menu drop-down list.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Select Settings.
4.  Select General.
5.  Select the Density drop-down list and select from the following options:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Default | Default displays elements on the screen such as content lists, form fields, buttons, etc. with a default amount of spacing between elements. |
| Comfortable | Comfortable displays elements on the screen with a medium amount of spacing between elements. For example, items are displayed closer together and with less space between them than the Default option. |
| Compact | Compact displays elements on the screen with a minimal amount of spacing between elements. For example, items are displayed closer together and with a minimal amount of space between them. |

## Composite Verification View Overview

The Composite Verification view is a combination of the following three panes which are displayed based on the status of the current batch.

1.  The Document List displays a list of all the documents inside the selected batch along with the thumbnails of the pages inside each document. For more information, refer to Working with the Document List.
2.  The Metadata Panel displays the form area for viewing and verifying document metadata for all the corresponding documents inside the batch which require a correction of the extraction results. For more information, refer to Working with the Metadata Panel.
3.  The Document Viewer window displays an enlarged version of the thumbnail that is selected in the Document List. For more information, refer to Working with the Document Viewer.

The Composite Verification View toolbar displays the current batch name and a progress bar indicating the percentage of completed documents in the batch. A document is considered to be complete when the document class is confirmed and the metadata fields are verified. The number of complete and incomplete documents are also displayed next to the progress bar.

## Endpoints

The Capture REST API provides access to the Export API's endpoints, which are described in the following sections. For more information on additional APIs that may be available, contact your solution provider.

-  Export

## Hxc Import Processor Compatibility

The Hyland Capture application is compatible with the Hyland Capture Import Processor (HxC Import Processor) module for OnBase Foundation EP4 and later.

The HxC Import Processor runs entirely within OnBase and imports documents that were captured in the Hyland Capture application. The imported documents are automatically indexed in OnBase using indexing information associated with the batch from the Hyland Capture application.

The HxC Import Processor can be configured to run automatically on a scheduled timer using the Unity Scheduler Service.

Note:
Starting with OnBase Foundation EP5, the HxC Import Processor can import unclassified documents into OnBase.

For complete information on configuring the HxC Import Processor, see the  Hyland Capture Import Processor module reference guide for OnBase.

## My Queues Overview

The My Queues page enables you to view a list of batches for the selected Content Queue, based on the environment you are associated with.

You can select the relevant Content Queue and subsequently view its batch lists. You can select the Show batches to be verified check box to filter the batch list so that only those batches that you can verify, are displayed.

A batch list is also your work list. You can select the batch with which you want to work and navigate directly to the Composite verification view.

You can perform the following actions on the Batch List page:

-
Selecting a Content Queue

-
Viewing the Batch List

-
Opening a batch for verification

-
Deleting a batch (if you have access to the Administration interface)

See the following sections for details:

-
Selecting a Content Queue

-
Viewing the Batch List

-
Deleting a Batch

## Objects

The following sections include objects common to multiple endpoints within the Capture REST API:

-  Export

## Opening The Hyland Capture Application

To open the Hyland Capture application from a supported web browser:

1.  Do one of the following:
-
Navigate to the application's URL.

Note:
If you navigate to a URL that contains an unauthorized environment ID for your account, a subscription error message is displayed when you log into the application. You can click Help Page to view this help document, or you can click Go To Hyland Experience Home to navigate to the Hyland Experience Capture portal and check your account settings. For assistance with your account's environment access, contact your system administrator.

-
From the Hyland Experience Administration Portal, select the appropriate environment and click Hyland Experience Capture.

Depending on your account and workstation settings, one of the following occurs:

-
If user credentials have been pre-configured and you are using your workstation's default browser, the Hyland Capture application is displayed. The remaining steps in this procedure do not apply.

Note:
If you have multiple tabs open, depending on your browser, the right-most tab may be displayed after you are automatically logged in to the Hyland Capture application.

-
If user credentials have not been pre-configured or you are not using your workstation's default browser, you are prompted to enter your Hyland Experience email address in your default browser.

2.  Enter your Hyland Experience email address in the field provided and click Continue. The application's login dialog box is displayed with your user name and email address automatically populated.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  If you need to switch to a different user account, do the following:
1.  Click the displayed email address. You are prompted to enter a different email address.
2.  Enter your email address in the field provided and click Submit. The application's login dialog box is displayed with your user name and email address automatically populated.
4.  Enter your password in the field provided. If you do not know your password, click Forgot Password? to reset it (see Resetting Your Password).
5.  To store your credentials locally, select Remember Me. This allows you to resume your session without logging in again when you need to reopen the Hyland Capture application.  Note:
If you are using a shared computer, selecting Remember Me is not recommended.

6.  Click Continue. The Hyland Capture application is displayed.
As you begin using the application, note the following:

-
If the latest major version of the Desktop Host is not running on your workstation, a message is displayed with a link to download the latest version. Once the updates are installed on your workstation, you must refresh the Hyland Capture application's page to continue. For assistance with installing the Desktop Host updates, contact your system administrator.

-
If you open the application in a non-default browser and log in using different credentials than the previous login, a message noting a user mismatch is displayed. To resolve the issue, close the login prompt window, refresh the application' s page, and log in using the correct credentials.

-
If your session times out after a period of inactivity (by default, 20 minutes), the Session Expiring dialog box is displayed. Click Stay Logged In to stay logged in to the application.

## Resizing Thumbnail Images

You can increase or decrease the size of the thumbnail images to optimize the view so that you can quickly work with the document, reorder the pages, split or append the document or perform some other action.

To resize thumbnail images, do one of the following:

-
Press Alt and + on the keyboard, to increase the size of the thumbnail images.

Note:
You can increase the size of the thumbnail images by two times their original size.

-
Press Alt and - on the keyboard, to reduce the size of the thumbnail images.

Note:
You can reduce the size of the thumbnail images to half of their original size.

## Running The Windows Installer

This section describes installing Desktop Host using the graphical installer for Windows. For illustration purposes, the following screenshots reference the 64-bit installer.

CAUTION:
Ensure that you use the 32-bit and 64-bit installers to install the application on 32-bit and 64-bit computers, respectively.

To install Desktop Host:

1.  Launch the Desktop Host installer by executing the installer (HylandDesktopHostSetup.x86.exe or HylandDesktopHostSetup.x64.exe).
The Welcome to Hyland Desktop Host Setup page is displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Click Install. If Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2019 Redistributable (x86 or x64) is not present, depending on your computer (32-bit or 64-bit), it is installed.
The Destination Folder page is displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  To change the installation location, enter a new folder location in the field provided or click Change to navigate to the folder location. The Change destination folder dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)    CAUTION:
Ensure that you choose a folder to which any user has full access (both read and write).

Note:
If the destination folder is not changed, the application is installed to C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost by default. The ProgramData folder is hidden by default in Windows; to view the folder you must configure Windows to show hidden items. For more information, see Microsoft's documentation on this.

4.  Enter the full path for the destination folder in the Folder name field provided or select the folder from the Look in drop-down list.
5.  Click Next. The File location for configuration settings page is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
6.  To specify the full path to the config.json file, select the Install configuration settings from the following location check box.  Note:
Selecting this option overwrites the config.json file in the installation directory. You can skip this step and provide the configuration settings after installation is complete.

7.  Enter the full path to the config.json file in the Configuration File Path field provided or click Browse.
8.  Click Next. The Ready to install page is displayed.
Click Back to return to the previous page to change configuration options, or click Cancel to close the installer without installing.

Note:
You may need to enter the Administrator password to continue installation.

9.  After the Installation completed page is displayed, click Close to complete the installation and exit the installer.

## Scan And Import Usage Overview

In the Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface, you can perform a batch capture process to capture image or PDF documents and prepare them for one of the following:

-
Later verification in the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface

-
Direct export out of the Hyland Capture application

While preparing to capture documents, you can also add, edit, or delete the input sources available for the batch capture process if you want to change your scanning devices or sweep directories from which to import documents.

See the following sections for details:

-
Capturing Documents

-
Working with Input Sources

-
Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export

-
Viewing Notifications

## Selecting A Content Queue

A Content Queue is a representation of batches grouped together by a similar business function. For example, a content queue for HR would have all HR related documents (Resumes, Sick Leave, Performance Reviews). They have associated classes (document types) that are specific to the content queue and represent the purpose of the batches being processed.

You can view a list of the available Content Queues in the Queue List pane on the left side of the batch list page. You can select the relevant Content Queue that you want to work with. When you select a content queue, the batches mapped to the selected content queue are displayed.

To select a content queue, select the relevant Content Queue from the Queue List drop-down list.

## Switching The Interface

While working in the Hyland Capture application, you can switch between the Scan and Import and Verify Data interfaces as needed to perform a batch capture process or verify a batch, respectively.

To switch the interface:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application).
2.  Click the navigation menu icon in the upper-left corner of the application to expand the navigation menu drop-down list.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Do one of the following:
-
To switch to the Scan and Import interface, select Scan and Import.

For information on performing a batch capture process, see Scan and Import Usage Overview.

-
To switch to the Verify Data interface, select Verify Data.

For information on verifying a batch, see Verify Data Usage Overview.

## Verify Data Usage Overview

The Hyland Experience Capture Verify Data interface enables you to validate and correct automatically processed incoming documents. You can perform manual classification of documents, correction of automatic classification and extraction results, and manual indexing of documents.

An intuitively structured user interface, sophisticated status management and filter techniques make this application easy-to-use and display only those documents that you need to check (currently, only PNG image documents are supported).

Keyboard shortcuts are available for most operations, enabling you to perform your tasks quickly. Through automatic locking, teams of users can safely process document batches.

See the following sections for details on these topics:

-
My Queues Overview

-
Composite Verification View Overview

-
Working with the Document List

-
Working with the Metadata Panel

-
Working with the Document Viewer

## Viewing Notifications

You can view notifications to check the availability of the latest version of the Desktop Host and the statuses of the batches that have been captured into your Hyland Capture system. While viewing these notifications, you can also download the latest version of the Desktop Host and discard any batches that failed to be fully captured.

Note:
Batch failures may be caused by a temporary issue with server processing or authentication. If the issue persists, contact your system administrator.

To view notifications:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed.  Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  Near the upper-right corner of the interface, click the Notifications icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Note:
If any notifications are associated with follow-up actions, a blue circle is displayed over the Notifications icon with the number of follow-up actions currently available.

The Notifications pane is displayed on the right side of the interface, showing the names and statuses of the batches that have been captured into the system recently. If the latest minor or patch version of the Desktop Host is not running on your workstation, a notification is displayed to indicate the availability of a newer version. When viewing the Notifications pane, note the following:

-
From the top of the pane, applicable notification types are displayed in the following order:

-
New Desktop Host version availability

-
Failed batches

-
Recently captured batches

-
Batches that have been deleted or successfully submitted to the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface are cleared from the Notifications pane after 60 seconds or after you refresh the browser.

3.  Click a notification's entry in the Notifications pane to expand the information displayed in the entry.
-
For a Desktop Host entry, a Download button is displayed.

-
For a batch entry, the batch's local ID is displayed. If the batch is in a failed state, a Discard button is also displayed.

4.  To download the latest minor or patch version of the Desktop Host, click Download. Once the updates are installed on your workstation, you must refresh the Hyland Capture application's page to use the updated version. For assistance with installing the Desktop Host updates, contact your system administrator.  Note: If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, it is highly recommended that you submit or discard the batch before installing Desktop Host updates. Installing Desktop Host updates while a batch is still pending review may prevent you from taking any further actions on the documents or pages in the batch.
5.  To discard a batch in a failed state, do the following:
1.  In the batch's expanded entry, click Discard. A confirmation message is displayed.
2.  Click Discard Batch to confirm.
6.  To hide the Notifications pane, click the Notifications icon or anywhere outside the pane in the Hyland Capture application.
